title: Registry schemas lack $ref to shared enum schemas
description: 15 framework-shipped registry schemas (architecture, audit, conformance-reference, decisions, domain, handoff, issues, phase, plan, project, rationale, requirements, roadmap, tasks, verification) carry inline enum strings for fields like priority / status / severity rather than $ref-ing the 6 canonical enum schemas landed at Step 4 (FGAP-016 surface). Drift risk: an enum value added to status.schema.json doesn't propagate to consumers; per-schema enum drift was the original FGAP-016 forcing condition.

evidence.reference: Inline enum for priority / status / severity / source rather than $ref to the 6 shared enum schemas under packages/pi-context/schemas/
Inline enum for status; no $id/version per FGAP-006 discipline
6 canonical shared enum schemas exist with $id pi-context://schemas/<name>; registry schemas don't reference them

impact: Enum-value additions to shared schemas don't propagate to consumer block schemas. The cross-block consistency that FGAP-016 surface was filed to enable is structurally unrealized for the 15 framework-shipped registry schemas. User-customized .project/schemas/ overrides also can't inherit enum updates from shared schemas.
proposed_resolution: Per-schema migration adding $id (pi-context://schemas/<name>) + version: '1.0.0' + $ref substitution for inline enums where applicable. Migration registry entries (per Step 4's schema-migrations.ts) author 1.0.0->1.1.0 transitions for any enum-value renames. Non-blocking for Step 5 install/lens-view surface — registry schemas carry forward verbatim in Step 5; this gap closure is its own future step (5b or later) that links the closing commit via resolved_by.
